---
title: "Stories"
format: html
---

# 📖 Stories: Faith in Action

Goal: To share real-world examples of how congregations are putting resilience and creation care into practice, allowing us to learn from each other’s successes and challenges. 

-----

## 💡 Inspiration for the Journey

Preparation is about more than technical steps; it’s about the people and communities leading the way. These stories highlight the practical application of our shared mission to protect God’s creation. 

-----

## 🌿 Mitigation: Lowering Our Footprint

Stories of congregations reducing their environmental impact and operational costs. 

### Solar Power in Practice: Christ Episcopal Church, Cedar Rapids, Iowa

After installing solar panels in July 2024, Christ Episcopal has seen a dramatic shift in its stewardship of resources. 

  - **The Impact:** Nearly two years later, church leaders report that the system is making a major impact on both their carbon footprint and their bottom line. 
  - **Financial Stewardship:** The transition has allowed the parish to save hundreds of dollars on monthly utility costs, redirecting those funds toward their core ministries. 
  - [Read the full story here](https://cbs2iowa.com/news/local/a-cedar-rapids-church-saves-hundreds-on-monthly-utility-costs-using-solar-power-system-christ-episcopal-church-solar-panels-energy-costs). 

<https://diosohio.org/installing-solar-at-good-shepherd-athens/> 

### Solar Powered School: St. Stephen's Episcopal School, Austin, Texas

In late 2022, **St. Stephen’s Episcopal School** in Austin, Texas, completed its
"Let the Sunshine In" initiative, installing 1,064 solar panels across its
370-acre campus to generate approximately **787,594 kWh** of clean energy
annually. Supported by lead donors and **Austin Energy** rebates, the project is
projected to save the school **$65,000** in yearly utility costs while doubling
as a "living laboratory" where students can study the science, ethics, and
economics of renewable energy. By integrating this infrastructure into both its
operations and its curriculum, St. Stephen’s has created a visible, long-term
witness to the Episcopal Church’s commitment to the **Care of Creation**,
modeling how institutional stewardship can foster both financial resilience and
environmental responsibility. [Read the full story
here](https://episcopalnewsservice.org/pressreleases/st-stephens-lets-the-sunshine-in/).

### Solar Hospitality: St. David's Episcopal Church, Austin, Texas

In an effort to align its historic urban presence with modern environmental stewardship, **St. David's Episcopal Church** in Austin, Texas, integrated a significant solar array as part of its major four-story facility expansion. By installing photovoltaic panels atop its downtown campus, the parish has successfully harnessed renewable energy to offset the high electrical demands of its large-scale hospitality spaces and commercial-grade kitchen. This commitment to solar power serves as a visible witness to creation care, demonstrating how historic congregations can utilize rooftops for climate mitigation while strengthening their long-term financial resilience through reduced utility expenditures. [Read the full story here](https://www.episcopalassetmap.org/dioceses/diocese-texas/list/st-davids-episcopal-church).

### Resilience From the Sun: St. Thomas the Apostle Episcopal Church, Nassau Bay, Texas

In a significant move toward institutional resilience, **St. Thomas the Apostle Episcopal Church** in Houston, Texas, has committed to installing a 52 kW solar array through a partnership with RE-volv, marking the 20th state reached by the nonprofit’s clean energy mission. Rev. Mike Stone describes the investment as a fundamental act of environmental stewardship that creates an "economy of scale" for the benefit of the local neighborhood. The system is projected to save the congregation over **$120,753** in energy costs, funds that will be directly reinvested into community outreach—specifically supporting the church’s food security initiatives that provide fresh food to more than 500 people each month. By leveraging solar power to reduce operational overhead, St. Thomas is effectively transforming renewable energy into tangible local impact, demonstrating how climate mitigation and social mission can work hand-in-hand. [Read the full story here](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/re-volv_expanding-solar-to-20-states-activity-7404265097533300738-e5vC/)


-----

## 😢 Adaptation: Building Resilience

Stories of hardening our facilities and caring for our neighbors during extreme events. 

### \[Placeholder: Your Story Here\] 

  - **The Challenge:** Has your parish faced a flood, freeze, or heatwave? 
  - **The Action:** What steps did you take to protect your building or serve as a community refuge? 
  - **The Lesson:** What is one thing you wish you had known before the event? 

-----

## 😇 Formation: Re-imagining Our Relationship

Stories of spiritual growth and community engagement with the natural world. 

### \[Placeholder: Your Story Here\] 

  - **The Practice:** Does your congregation host a "Book Club," a "Liturgy in the Garden," or a "Creation Walk"? 
  - **The Transformation:** How has focusing on stewardship changed the way your members view their faith and their local ecosystem? 

-----

## 📢 Share Your Narrative

We want to spread stories of both successes and failures to all our congregations. Sharing your experience helps your "fellow travelers" better relate to the work of resilience. 

If you have a story, a photo, or a lesson learned to share, please contact Alan Jackson or Ro.
